reachability_artifacts: cmd/sworn/route_test.go — TestRouteIntegration runs sworn route against a fixture repo covering every state branch

design_decisions.choice: OracleReader interface defined in internal/board with router-friendly signatures (ReadSliceStatus, ReadBoard hiding ref/track-map resolution).
options: Pass *Oracle directly with full params to Route
design_decisions.human_decision: Define router-friendly interface in internal/board; CLI constructs Oracle+reader, passes interface to router.
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the router testable with fakes; hides git-ref resolution from the pure decision function.

design_decisions.choice: LastCommitTime added to internal/git/git.go as method on *Repo wrapping `git log -1 --format=%ct`.
options: Define in router package with os/exec
design_decisions.human_decision: Add to *git.Repo; also add IsAncestor. Both declared in planned_files.
design_decisions.rationale: git.Repo is the single git-operation surface; new methods are additive (no existing method modified).

design_decisions.choice: CommitTimeReader is a separate interface in internal/router for commit-time queries; do NOT add LastCommitTime to board.gitContentReader.
design_decisions.human_decision: Separate CommitTimeReader interface. board.gitContentReader is the board-oracle contract; adding LastCommitTime breaks cmd/sworn/board.go's oracleReader.
design_decisions.rationale: Interface segregation: board oracle reads state, router queries commit times. Different concerns, different interfaces.

design_decisions.choice: Design-review routing: approved-ack.md via CatFileExists (committed-ref); design/review/decline via LastCommitTime on track branch.
options: CatFileExists for approved-ack.md; LastCommitTime for others (committed-ref, stricter)
os.Stat + git log -1 matching bash script exactly
design_decisions.human_decision: CatFileExists for approved-ack.md; LastCommitTime for others. Committed-ref check; slightly stricter than bash (uncommitted approved-ack.md → absent).
design_decisions.rationale: Router reads committed state; avoids working-tree dependency. Stricter means uncommitted approved-ack is invisible — implementer must commit after coach ack.

design_decisions.choice: verification.reason: use strings.Join(violations, "; ") as fallback; no Reason field added to state.Verification.
design_decisions.human_decision: Use strings.Join(violations, "; ") only. No status.json has ever set verification.reason; the jq // fallback was never populated in practice.
design_decisions.rationale: No production data carries the field; adding it to state.Verification is spec fiction. The bash jq is pure defensive fallback.
